description_long: This stone object is irregularly shaped with a rugged, highly textured surface, featuring multiple protrusions and deep crevices. Its coloration is predominantly a mix of earthy browns and grays, giving it a natural, weathered appearance. The stone has a bulky, asymmetrical form, with one side rising higher and forming a jagged peak, while the opposite side is broader and flatter. Several cavities and indentations are visible, suggesting erosion or natural wear over time. The surface is matte and rough, with no visible signs of artificial modification or polish. This stone would likely serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A large, irregularly shaped stone with a rough, textured surface and earthy brown-gray coloration. It features deep crevices, jagged peaks, and natural weathering.
description_view_0: Tall, narrow face with a prominent vertical ridge and deep surface texture.
description_view_1: Broad, flat side showing the stone's full length and rough, uneven surface.
description_view_2: Angled view highlighting a large cavity and the stone's irregular, sloping form.
description_view_3: Rear view with multiple depressions and a rugged, blocky profile.
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and pitting with no regular pattern; natural weathering marks dominate the surface.
attribute_weathering: Rough and eroded with visible cavities and surface pitting, indicating prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured lawns or paved surfaces, but may blend with other natural stones or vegetation in landscaped settings.
